title: Intelligence Efficiency, Ben Geist | Compile 26
summary: Ben Geist, Research Engineer at Ramp, argues that intelligence efficiency improves through better context and entropy reduction rather than simply scaling compute. He discusses token spend patterns and contrasts computational work with contextual understanding in making models more efficient.
writeup: Ben Geist, a Research Engineer at Ramp, makes the case that intelligence efficiency comes from better context and entropy reduction rather than from scaling compute alone. The talk examines token spend patterns and draws a contrast between computational work and contextual understanding as routes to more efficient models. The central takeaway: improving how models acquire and use context, and reducing entropy, can raise efficiency without proportional increases in compute.
